what_it_does: A small global-window React component rendering a horizontal action toolbar — Select, Filter, Sort buttons, a search input, a Clear Filters button, and right-aligned Create New / Upload buttons — built from inline SVG path icons and a generic button helper.
observations: Defines a terse single-letter icon component I({d, fill}) that maps an array of path 'd' strings into <path> elements
Icons object holds path-data arrays for select/filter/sort/clear/plus/upload/search
ToolbarBtn switches between cv-btn--primary and cv-btn--outline classes by a 'primary' prop
Search field renders its own inline magnifier SVG (circle + path) separate from Icons.search
Props (showSelect, showCreate, showUpload, search, onSearch) toggle visibility and wire the controlled input
Only the search input is wired to a handler; the other buttons have no onClick behaviour
standouts: All iconography is inline SVG path data rather than an icon font or asset, keeping the component dependency-free
conventions: Global-window component registration (no imports/exports) consistent with the Babel-in-browser kit pattern
Styling delegated entirely to cv-* classes (defined in platform.css) — no inline style except one stroke color via var(--fg-muted)
Uses CSS custom property var(--fg-muted) for the search icon stroke, respecting the token system
concerns: Filter/Sort/Clear/Create/Upload buttons are inert (no onClick) — presentational only in this kit
Two near-duplicate search-magnifier SVGs exist (Icons.search vs the inline one used in render)
notes: If briefing someone: this is the presentational toolbar row for the platform dashboard kit — only search is interactive; the rest are visual placeholders styled by platform.css.
questions: Are the non-search buttons meant to stay inert in this kit, or wired up by a consumer?
Why does Icons.search exist if the render uses a separately inlined magnifier SVG?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the header comment lists Select/Filter/Sort/Search/Clear/Create/Upload and the component renders exactly those.
intention_signals: Aims to provide a reusable, configurable toolbar row for list/gallery screens in the creator-side platform dashboard, depicting the standard actions a content collection needs.
contribution: Contributes the platform dashboard's action-toolbar component and its set of inline SVG action icons (select/filter/sort/clear/plus/upload/search).
summary_for_embedding: A global-window React ActionToolbar component for the platform UI kit with inline SVG icons for select, filter, sort, search, clear, create, and upload.
intention_for_embedding: To give creator-side dashboard list/gallery screens a reusable, prop-configurable toolbar of standard collection actions.
